content_en:
When * Umar first became the Caliph, he declared: "I am entitled to only two garments to be paid for from the Treasury, one in winter and another in summer, as well as provisions for my household similar to the provisions of an average man from Quraysh who is not one of their wealthiest. I am only an ordinary Muslim."

It is also related that “Umar, may Allah be pleased with him, was once late for the Friday congregational salah. When he arrived at the mosque, he went up the pulpit and apologized to people saying: "I was held back because of this garment of mine. It is the only one I have got and it was being sewn." The garment was white with the sleeves hardly reaching his wrists. (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.)

Jabir Ton * Abdillah mentions: "* Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him, saw me carrying some meat.

He asked me, ‘What is this, Jabir?' | answered, 'I hungered for meat so I bought some.' “Umar returned: 'Do you go out and buy whatever you hunger for?! Do you not worry about what this verse tells you:

(he ed ty ie

"Ye received your good things in the life of the world" ?' "(A\- Ahgaf: 20)

It is recounted that Hafs Ibn Abul--Aas used to be present when ~Umar ate, yet never touched the food. ~ Umar asked him, "Why is it that you never join us?" He replied, "The food you eat is rough and coarse. Back home, I will find good food prepared for me." ~Umar returned: "Do you think | am incapable of ordering a ewe to be skinned, flour to be sifted and placed in a piece of cloth then baked into tender bread, and amounts of raisins to be soaked in water." Hafs wondered, "I can see you are well-aware of what a luxurious life is all about!" ~Umar answered: "I am. I swear by Him in Whose Hands my soul is, had I not been worried that the reward for my good deeds might diminish, I would have joined you in this life of yours." (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.)

~Umar was once admonished and told: "If you eat good food, you will be more capable of discharging your duties." *“Umar returned: “My two companions followed a certain course in life. Should I digress from it, I will not enjoin a status equal to theirs."

During the Ramadah Year' (i.e. the Ash Year), ~ Umar

'“Aam Ar-Ramaadah ov the Ramaadah Year refers to the time when Arabs were

afflicted with a severe famine that lasted for almost cight months, and during which “Umar was an admirable example of austerity.

lived on bread and oil till his skin darkened. He used to say: "Damned would I be as a ruler, if I ate to my heart's content while people went hungry."

True are indeed the words of Talhah Ibn * Ubayd when he said: "“Umar was not the first among us to embrace Islam, nor the first to migrate. Yet, he outdid us all in renouncing worldly pleasures and was certainly the keenest on winning Allah's reward in the Hereafter." (Reported by [bn ~ Aasaakir.)

Though the Caliph, who was on top of an incessantly expanding state, “Umar [bn Al-Khattaab was a living example of humbleness not only to the poorest and humblest of his subjects, but also for entire generations of Muslims up to our present time.

Qatadah states: “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, may Allah be pleased with him, stepped out of the mosque with Al-Jarood. He saw an old woman sitting out on the street. Umar greeted her, the woman answered his greetings. She then said: "Oh, “Umar! 1 was there when you used to be called 'Little *Umar' at Soog ~Ukaaz (the *Ukaaz market), when you would brawl with little boys. Days passed by and you came to be called “Umar, and again days passed by till you came to be called the Commander of the Faithful. So, fear Allah when dealing with your subjects and remember that whoever fears death avoids sin. “Umar wept. Al-Jarood intervened: " Hey, you had the audacity to reduce the Commander of the Faithful to tears!" “Umar returned: "It is all right. Do you not know who she is? She is Khawlah Bint Hakeem whom Allah, high in the Heavens, heard complain. This is all the more reason why *Umar should listen to her words." (Quoted from Al-Misbah.)

*“Umar stepped out of his house in the hush of the night and was spotted by Talhah. ~Umar walked into a house and then headed for another. The next morning, Talhah made it to that house where he found a blind crippled old woman. He asked her: "Why does that man come to your house?" She answered: "He takes care of me; brings me what I need and guards me against harm." Talhah thought to himself: "May your mother be bereaved of you, Tulahah! Are you trying to find fault with * Umar?" (Reported by Abu Na‘ eem.)

A delegation from Iraq, among whom was Al-Ahnaf [bn Qays, came to meet * Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab on a blistering hot summer day. Shielding his head with a cloak, ~ Umar was busy covering the charity camels with tar. He called out: "Take off your clothes, Al-Ahnaf and come help the Commander of the Faithful with this camel for it is one of the charity camels, that is, the rights of orphans, widows and poor people." One of the delegates said: "May Allah forgive you, Commander of the Faithful, can you not just order one of the charity bondmen to do the job?" ‘Umar replied: "Who is more enslaved than myself or Al-Ahnaf? Whoever is in charge of Muslims’ affairs owes his subjects exactly what a bondman owes his master, namely offering sound advice and performing his duty with all honesty." (Quoted from Akhbaar *Umar.)

~“Umar was reported to have entered Jerusalem riding a workhorse, which kept swaggering. “Umar started lashing it with his outfit and then said: "May whoever taught you this be damned! This is a sign of arrogance." “Umar then dismounted it and said: "You had me carried on a devil's back. Once ] dismounted, I had no feeling of ostentation. (Reported by AtTabari.)

It is also narrated that “Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab called out: "Come to salah in congregation." “Umar went up the pulpit and

sat where Abu Bakr used to place his feet. When people gathered, *Umar stood up. He started off by praising Allah and glorifying Him, as befits the Lord. He then invoked peace and blessings on the Prophet. ~Umar proceeded saying: "I saw in my dream that I was shepherding the sheep of my aunts of the Banu Makhzoom tribe. They handed me a handful of dates and raisins, which sustained me for the whole day, tough as they may be." When *Umar finished, he stepped down the pulpit. *Abdur-Rahmaan_ Ibn * Awf told him: "You insist on censuring yourself, Commander of the Faithful?" ~Umar replied: "Woe unto you, Ibn *Awf! When I am on my own, I think: ‘I am the Commander of the Faithful. Who could possibly be better than me?’ That is why I mean to show myself who I really am.” (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.)

Al-Hasan reports: "‘Umar went out ona hot summer day, with his garment pulled over his head. A young boy riding on a donkey passed him by, ‘Umar called out: "Take me along, boy.' The boy jumped off his donkey and_ said: 'Get on it, Commander of the Faithful!' * Umar returned: "No, you do, and 1 will ride behind you. You want me to sit on the soft part while you sit on the hard part!" Thus, * Umar rode into the city sitting behind the boy, hence causing all people to stare." (Quoted from Hayatus-Sahabah, The Lives of the Companians.)

Abu Mahdhoorah states: "I was at *Umar Ibn AL Khattaab's, may Allah be pleased with him, when Safawaan Ibn ‘Umayyah brought a bowl wrapped ina cloak. It was placed before *Umar. ~Umar called a host of poor people to join him, then said: "Damned are those who refuse to eat with poor people!’ Safawaan returned: 'It is not that we refuse to let them join us; I swear by Allah that we simply cannot afford fine food both for ourselves and them, too,' " (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)

There is no better evidence of his indisputable

humbleness than his story with Al-Hurmudhaan (a Persian king) after Muslims' conquering Persia. A delegation of Muslims, among whom were Anas Ibn Malik, Al-Ahnaf Ibn Qays along with Al-Hurmudhaan arrived at Madinah bringing one-fifth of the booty. They walked in the city, asked to be directed to the house of the Commander of the Faithful, where they found no one. The delegation thereupon turned and left. On their way. they came across some boys playing so they asked them where they could find “Umar. The boys answered: "He is asleep at the mosque, resting his head on his hooded cloak. The delegation headed for the mosque where they did find ~ Umar asleep, with his head resting on his hooded cloak, which he had put on to receive the delegation in, and with his Dirah in his hand. There was no one else in the mosque. Al-Hurmudhaan asked, "Where is Umar?" Muslims replied, "There he is." People whispered to one another so as not to wake him up. Yet, Al-Hurmudhaan kept wondering, "Where are his chamberlains?! Where are his guards?!" Muslims answered, "He has got no chamberlains, no guards, no scribe and no Diwan (consultative assembly)." AlHurmudhaan returned, "He must be a Prophet!" Muslims returned; "No, but he acts like one." (Reported by Ibn Katheer.)

*Umar Follows the Example of the Prophet:

“Umar spared no effort in following the example of the Prophet, peace and blessings be upon him, and walking closely in his footsteps. History abounds in instances where he could not have been keener on abiding by the Prophet's Tradition.

*Atikah Bint Zayd Ibn ~ Amr, *Umar's wife, mentions that whenever she asked ~Umar's permission to go to the mosque, he would be silent. ~ Umar used to tell her: "You know that I hate that you go out." ‘Umar was a man of enormous Ghayrah (i.e. he was very protective of his women out of self-

respect). His wife would return: "I swear by Allah I will go out unless you prevent me." Yet, ‘Umar never did. (Reported by Malik.) Even the day ~Umar was stabbed, his wife was in the mosque.

In another narration: It is reported that ~Umar's wife used to perform the morning (Fajr) and the night salah (*Ishaa') in congregation at the mosque. She was asked: "Why do you go out when you know he hates that on account of his Ghairah?" *“Umar's wife replied: "Why can he not forbid me to?" They answered: “He would never do because of the Prophet's saying: ‘If your women ask permission to 80 to the mosque at night, allow them." " (Reported by Al-Bukhari.)
content_id:
Ketika * Umar pertama kali menjadi Khalifah, ia menyatakan: "Aku hanya berhak atas dua pakaian yang dibayar dari Baitul Mal, satu untuk musim dingin dan satu untuk musim panas, serta jatah makan untuk keluargaku yang serupa dengan jatah makan orang biasa dari Quraysh yang bukan termasuk orang terkaya mereka. Aku hanyalah seorang Muslim biasa."

Diriwayatkan pula bahwa 'Umar, radhiyallahu 'anhu, pernah terlambat menghadiri salah Jum'at. Ketika tiba di masjid, ia naik ke mimbar dan meminta maaf kepada jamaah seraya berkata: "Aku tertahan karena pakaianku ini. Inilah satu-satunya yang kumiliki dan sedang dijahit." Pakaian itu berwarna putih dengan lengan yang hampir tidak sampai ke pergelangan tangannya. (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Sa'd.)

Jabir Ibn 'Abdillah menceritakan: "'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, radhiyallahu 'anhu, melihatku sedang membawa daging.

Ia bertanya kepadaku, 'Apa ini, Jabir?' Aku menjawab, 'Aku ingin makan daging maka aku membelinya.' 'Umar berkata: 'Apakah kamu pergi membeli apa saja yang kamu inginkan?! Tidakkah kamu khawatir dengan apa yang dinyatakan ayat ini kepadamu:

(he ed ty ie

"Kamu telah menghabiskan kenikmatan kalian dalam kehidupan dunia" ?' "(Al-Ahqaf: 20)

Diceritakan bahwa Hafs Ibn Abul-Aas selalu hadir ketika 'Umar makan, namun tidak pernah menyentuh makanan. 'Umar bertanya kepadanya, "Mengapa kamu tidak pernah makan bersama kami?" Ia menjawab, "Makanan yang Anda makan kasar dan keras. Di rumah, aku akan mendapati makanan yang enak telah tersedia untukku." 'Umar berkata: "Apakah kamu kira aku tidak mampu memerintahkan seekor domba untuk disembelih, tepung untuk diayak dan dibungkus dengan kain lalu dipanggang menjadi roti yang lembut, serta sejumlah kismis untuk direndam dalam air?" Hafs heran, "Kulihat Anda sangat mengenal bagaimana gaya hidup mewah itu!" 'Umar menjawab: "Benar. Demi Dia yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, seandainya aku tidak khawatir pahala amal baikku akan berkurang, niscaya aku akan bergabung denganmu dalam kehidupan seperti itu." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Sa'd.)

'Umar pernah dinasihati dan diberitahu: "Jika Anda makan makanan yang baik, Anda akan lebih mampu menjalankan tugas-tugas Anda." 'Umar menjawab: "Dua sahabatku menempuh jalan hidup tertentu. Jika aku menyimpang darinya, aku tidak akan mencapai kedudukan yang setara dengan mereka."

Pada Tahun Ramadah' (yakni Tahun Abu), 'Umar

'Aam Ar-Ramaadah atau Tahun Ramadah merujuk pada masa ketika bangsa Arab ditimpa kelaparan parah yang berlangsung hampir delapan bulan, dan selama itu 'Umar menjadi teladan mengagumkan dalam hal kesederhanaan.

hidup dengan roti dan minyak hingga kulitnya menghitam. Ia kerap berkata: "Celakalah aku sebagai penguasa, jika aku makan sepuasnya sementara rakyat kelaparan."

Sungguh benar kata-kata Thalhah Ibn 'Ubayd ketika ia berkata: "'Umar bukanlah yang pertama di antara kami memeluk Islam, bukan pula yang pertama berhijrah. Namun, ia mengungguli kami semua dalam meninggalkan kesenangan duniawi dan sungguh yang paling bersemangat meraih pahala Allah di akhirat." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu 'Aasaakir.)

Meskipun sebagai Khalifah yang memimpin negara yang terus berkembang, 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab adalah contoh hidup tentang kerendahan hati, tidak hanya bagi rakyatnya yang termiskin dan paling hina, tetapi juga bagi seluruh generasi Muslim hingga masa kini.

Qatadah menyatakan: "'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, radhiyallahu 'anhu, keluar dari masjid bersama Al-Jarood. Ia melihat seorang wanita tua duduk di pinggir jalan. 'Umar menyapa wanita itu, ia pun membalas salamnya. Lalu ia berkata: "Wahai 'Umar! Aku ada di sana ketika kamu dipanggil 'Umar Kecil' di Souq 'Ukaaz (pasar 'Ukaaz), ketika kamu berkelahi dengan anak-anak kecil. Hari demi hari berlalu hingga kamu dipanggil 'Umar, dan kembali hari berlalu hingga kamu disebut Amirul Mukminin. Maka, bertakwalah kepada Allah dalam mengurusi rakyatmu dan ingatlah bahwa siapa yang takut mati ia menjauhi dosa." 'Umar menangis. Al-Jarood menyela: "Hei, kamu telah berani membuat Amirul Mukminin menangis!" 'Umar menjawab: "Tidak mengapa. Tidakkah kamu tahu siapa dia? Dia adalah Khawlah Binti Hakeem yang keluhannya didengar Allah di langit yang tinggi. Ini justru semakin menguatkan alasan bagi 'Umar untuk mendengar perkataannya." (Dikutip dari Al-Misbah.)

'Umar keluar dari rumahnya di keheningan malam dan diperhatikan oleh Thalhah. 'Umar masuk ke sebuah rumah lalu menuju ke rumah lain. Keesokan paginya, Thalhah mendatangi rumah itu dan mendapati seorang wanita tua yang buta dan lumpuh. Ia bertanya padanya: "Mengapa orang itu datang ke rumahmu?" Ia menjawab: "Ia merawatku; membawakan apa yang aku butuhkan dan melindungiku dari bahaya." Thalhah berpikir dalam hati: "Semoga ibumu berduka atasmu, wahai Thalhah! Apakah kamu hendak mencari kesalahan 'Umar?" (Diriwayatkan oleh Abu Na'eem.)

Sebuah delegasi dari Iraq, di antaranya Al-Ahnaf Ibn Qays, datang menemui 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab pada hari musim panas yang terik. Dengan melindungi kepalanya menggunakan jubah, 'Umar sibuk menutupi unta-unta zakat dengan ter. Ia berseru: "Lepaskan pakaianmu, wahai Al-Ahnaf, dan bantulah Amirul Mukminin mengurus unta ini karena unta itu adalah salah satu unta zakat, yaitu hak para yatim, janda, dan orang-orang miskin." Salah seorang delegasi berkata: "Semoga Allah mengampunimu, wahai Amirul Mukminin, tidakkah Anda bisa memerintahkan salah satu hamba sahaya zakat untuk melakukannya?" 'Umar menjawab: "Siapa yang lebih hamba daripada aku atau Al-Ahnaf? Siapa pun yang mengurusi urusan kaum Muslimin berutang kepada rakyatnya tepat seperti yang seorang hamba berutang kepada tuannya, yaitu memberikan nasihat yang tulus dan menjalankan tugasnya dengan sepenuh kejujuran." (Dikutip dari Akhbaar 'Umar.)

'Umar dilaporkan pernah memasuki Yerusalem dengan menunggangi seekor kuda kerja yang terus berlenggak-lenggok. 'Umar mulai memecut kuda itu dengan pakaiannya lalu berkata: "Terkutuklah siapa pun yang mengajarimu ini! Ini adalah tanda kesombongan." 'Umar kemudian turun dari kuda itu dan berkata: "Kamu telah membawaku menunggangi punggung setan. Begitu aku turun, aku tidak merasakan perasaan sombong itu lagi." (Diriwayatkan oleh At-Thabari.)

Diriwayatkan pula bahwa 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab berseru: "Marilah mendirikan salah berjamaah." 'Umar naik ke mimbar dan

duduk di tempat di mana Abu Bakr biasa meletakkan kakinya. Ketika orang-orang berkumpul, 'Umar berdiri. Ia memulai dengan memuji Allah dan mengagungkan-Nya sebagaimana layak bagi Sang Rabb. Kemudian ia bershalawat atas Nabi. 'Umar melanjutkan dengan berkata: "Aku bermimpi bahwa aku sedang menggembala domba-domba bibi-bibiku dari suku Banu Makhzoom. Mereka memberiku segenggam kurma dan kismis yang menopangku sepanjang hari, meskipun keras adanya." Setelah 'Umar selesai, ia turun dari mimbar. 'Abdurrahman Ibn 'Awf berkata kepadanya: "Anda terus-menerus mencela diri sendiri, wahai Amirul Mukminin?" 'Umar menjawab: "Celaka engkau, wahai Ibnu 'Awf! Ketika aku sendirian, aku berpikir: 'Akulah Amirul Mukminin. Siapa yang mungkin lebih baik dariku?' Itulah mengapa aku bermaksud menunjukkan kepada diriku sendiri siapa aku sebenarnya." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Sa'd.)

Al-Hasan melaporkan: "'Umar keluar pada suatu hari musim panas yang terik, dengan pakaiannya ditarik menutupi kepalanya. Seorang anak laki-laki yang menunggangi keledai melewatinya. 'Umar berseru: 'Boncengkan aku, nak.' Anak itu melompat turun dari keledainya dan berkata: 'Silakan naik, wahai Amirul Mukminin!' 'Umar menjawab: 'Tidak, kamu yang naik, dan aku akan duduk di belakangmu. Kamu mau aku duduk di bagian yang empuk sementara kamu duduk di bagian yang keras!' Maka 'Umar pun masuk ke kota dengan duduk di belakang anak itu, sehingga membuat semua orang terpana." (Dikutip dari Hayatus-Shahabah, Kehidupan Para Sahabat.)

Abu Mahdhoorah menyatakan: "Aku sedang berada di tempat 'Umar Ibn Al-Khattaab, radhiyallahu 'anhu, ketika Shafwan Ibn 'Umayyah membawa sebuah mangkuk yang dibungkus dengan jubah. Mangkuk itu diletakkan di hadapan 'Umar. 'Umar memanggil sejumlah orang miskin untuk bergabung bersamanya, lalu berkata: 'Terkutuklah mereka yang menolak makan bersama orang-orang miskin!' Shafwan menjawab: 'Bukan berarti kami menolak membiarkan mereka bergabung; demi Allah, kami memang tidak mampu menyediakan makanan yang baik untuk diri kami sendiri sekaligus untuk mereka.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)

Tidak ada bukti yang lebih baik dari kerendahan hatinya yang tak terbantahkan

selain kisahnya dengan Al-Hurmuzhaan (seorang raja Persia) setelah kaum Muslimin menaklukkan Persia. Sebuah delegasi Muslim, di antaranya Anas Ibn Malik, Al-Ahnaf Ibn Qays bersama Al-Hurmuzhaan, tiba di Madinah membawa seperlima harta rampasan perang. Mereka berjalan memasuki kota, bertanya arah menuju rumah Amirul Mukminin, namun tidak menemukan seorang pun di sana. Delegasi kemudian berbalik dan pergi. Di tengah perjalanan, mereka bertemu dengan anak-anak yang sedang bermain sehingga mereka bertanya di mana mereka bisa menemukan 'Umar. Anak-anak menjawab: "Ia sedang tidur di masjid, menyandarkan kepalanya pada jubah berkapnya." Delegasi itu menuju masjid dan benar-benar mendapati 'Umar sedang tidur, dengan kepalanya bersandar pada jubah berkap yang telah ia kenakan untuk menyambut delegasi, serta tongkatnya di tangannya. Tidak ada seorang pun di masjid selainnya. Al-Hurmuzhaan bertanya, "Di mana 'Umar?" Kaum Muslimin menjawab, "Itulah dia." Orang-orang berbisik satu sama lain agar tidak membangunkannya. Namun, Al-Hurmuzhaan terus bertanya-tanya, "Di mana para penjaga pintunya?! Di mana para pengawalnya?!" Kaum Muslimin menjawab, "Ia tidak memiliki penjaga pintu, tidak ada pengawal, tidak ada juru tulis, dan tidak ada Diwan (majelis musyawarah)." Al-Hurmuzhaan berkata, "Ia pasti seorang Nabi!" Kaum Muslimin menjawab: "Bukan, tetapi ia bertindak seperti salah satu dari mereka." (Diriwayatkan oleh Ibnu Katsir.)

*Umar Mengikuti Teladan Nabi:

'Umar tidak pernah menyia-nyiakan kesempatan untuk mengikuti teladan Nabi, shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam, dan berjalan mengikuti jejaknya dengan seksama. Sejarah penuh dengan contoh-contoh di mana ia tidak bisa lebih bersungguh-sungguh dalam berpegang teguh pada Sunnah Nabi.

*Atikah binti Zayd ibn 'Amr, istri 'Umar, menyebutkan bahwa setiap kali ia meminta izin kepada 'Umar untuk pergi ke masjid, ia selalu diam. 'Umar biasa berkata kepadanya: "Engkau tahu bahwa aku tidak suka engkau keluar." 'Umar adalah seorang yang memiliki Ghayrah yang sangat besar (yakni ia sangat melindungi wanita-wanitanya karena rasa harga diri).

Istrinya kemudian menjawab: "Demi Allah, aku akan tetap keluar kecuali engkau melarangku." Namun 'Umar tidak pernah melakukannya. (Diriwayatkan oleh Malik.) Bahkan pada hari 'Umar ditikam, istrinya sedang berada di masjid.

Dalam riwayat lain: Disebutkan bahwa istri 'Umar biasa melaksanakan salah Subuh (Fajr) dan salah malam ('Isya') secara berjamaah di masjid. Ia ditanya: "Mengapa engkau tetap keluar padahal engkau tahu ia tidak menyukainya karena Ghayrah-nya?" Istri 'Umar menjawab: "Mengapa ia tidak melarangku?" Mereka menjawab: "Ia tidak akan pernah melakukan itu karena sabda Nabi: 'Jika wanita-wanita kalian meminta izin untuk pergi ke masjid pada malam hari, izinkanlah mereka.'" (Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhari.)
content_ar: عندما * عمر أولاً became the Caliph, he declared: "I am entitled to only two garments to be paid for from the Treasury, one in winter and another in summer, as well as provisions for my household similar to the provisions of an average man from Quraysh who is not one of their wealthiest. I am only an ordinary مسلم." It is أيضاً related that “عمر, may الله be pleased with him, was حين late for the Friday congregational الصلاة. عندما he arrived at the المسجد, he went up the pulpit and apologized to people saying: "I was held back لأن of this garment of mine. It is the only one I have got and it was being sewn." The garment was white with the sleeves hardly reaching his wrists. (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.) Jabir Ton * Abdillah mentions: "* عمر Ibn Al-Khattaab, may الله be pleased with him, saw me carrying some meat. He asked me, ‘What is this, Jabir?' | answered, 'I hungered for meat لذا I bought some.' “عمر returned: 'Do you go out and buy whatever you hunger for?! Do you not worry about what this الآية tells you: (he ed ty ie "Ye received your الخير things in the الحياة of the الدنيا" ?' "(A\- Ahgaf: 20) It is recounted that Hafs Ibn Abul--Aas used to be present عندما ~عمر ate, yet never touched the food. ~ عمر asked him, "Why is it that you never join us?" أجاب, "The food you eat is rough and coarse. Back home, I will find الخير food prepared for me." ~عمر returned: "Do you think | am incapable of ordering a ewe to be skinned, flour to be sifted and placed in a piece of cloth then baked into tender bread, and amounts of raisins to be soaked in water." Hafs wondered, "I can see you are well-aware of what a luxurious الحياة is all about!" ~عمر answered: "I am. I swear by Him in Whose Hands my النفس is, had I not been worried that the الأجر for my الخير deeds might diminish, I would have joined you in this الحياة of yours." (Reported by Ibn Sa*d.) ~عمر was حين admonished and told: "إذا you eat الخير food, you will be more capable of discharging your duties." *“عمر returned: “My two الصحابة followed a certain course in الحياة. Should I digress from it, I will not enjoin a status equal to theirs." خلال the Ramadah Year' (i.e. the Ash Year), ~ عمر '“Aam Ar-Ramaadah ov the Ramaadah Year refers to the time عندما Arabs were afflicted with a severe famine that lasted for almost cight months, and خلال which “عمر was an admirable example of austerity. lived on bread and oil till his skin darkened. He used to say: "Damned would I be as a ruler, إذا I ate to my القلب's content بينما people went hungry." True are indeed the words of Talhah Ibn * Ubayd عندما قال: "“عمر was not the أولاً among us to embrace الإسلام, nor the أولاً to migrate. Yet, he outdid us all in renouncing worldly pleasures and was certainly the keenest on winning الله's الأجر in الآخرة." (Reported by [bn ~ Aasaakir.)
